title: Secura Electric Coffee Grinder and Spice Grinder with 2 Stainless Steel Blades Removable Bowls
description:
About this item
Multi-Use - Ideal for grinding coffee, spices, herbs, nuts, and even grains—this versatile grinder meets all your kitchen needs
Removable Grinding Bowls – Comes with two separate bowls for coffee and spices, ensuring easy cleanup and preventing flavor mixing
Press Operation - Simple, user-friendly press control makes it easy to grind with precision, whether you prefer a coarse or fine grind
Overheat Protection – Automatically shuts off if the motor gets too hot, preventing damage and extending the grinder's lifespan
Notice - Avoid grinding high water content and extremely hard foods like cinnamon, cloves, or coconut, as they may doing damage to the motor, the lid, or cause other issues
reviews_5s:
I bought it for two purposes, grinding small portions of chutney, and grind small portions of Indian species. It does both very well (chutney comes out pretty even and spices are grinded just fine, but not ultra fine which is ok). It is serving as a smart and quick assistant in our kitchen. We can now smell fresh spices. I think it won't do heavy duty work, and and we have a separate machine for that and we never tried on it. We don't dish wash it (just as a preference as blades can get blunt over time in dishwasher and blades on this one are not separate). We love the steel jar provided, it is quick and easy to clean. Small rinse is enough, though we seldom clean grinder (it is not needed for same set of spices). We have not tried coffee as we are not regular coffee drinkers.Products are well made. Double bowls are a great advantage and I can have a dedicated bowl for spices.
This company cares about the service provided and this US company stands by their designs and product function .I purchased this grinder for the purpose of grinding up dried herbs from my garden. I ground up rosemary, which is a very tough plant, sage and oregano. There are 2 cups - one for a coarse grind, one for finer grind. I tried both. I was able to grind up my herbs to fine powder (which is what I wanted)…but could have done a coarser grind with the other cup or less time. It was easy to use, easy to clean and takes up minimal space in my cupboard. Good value for the money.Been using this for the past month on both wet and dry ingredients and it has been working well so far. Very low noise and quick. Tried grinding pepper in the dry jar and it worked perfectly within a couple minutes. I did notice that the base got hot by the time the pepper was finely ground. Used the wet jar for ginger, garlic and coconut and it did a very good job. Very useful item to have in your kitchenI recently got the Secura Spice Grinder, and it’s been a fantastic addition to my kitchen! This grinder is powerful and easily grinds everything from whole peppercorns to dried herbs. The adjustable settings let me choose the coarseness I want, whether I’m prepping a quick dinner or making a special recipe. There are two jars, one for dry grinding and one for wet.
I also appreciate how easy it is to clean—just a quick rinse, and it’s good to go! The design is sleek and compact, fitting perfectly on my countertop without taking up too much spaceI’m an Asian and we prepare lunch and dinner almost daily. We need lots of spice grinding and mixing for Indian curry. It is perfect for making tomato sauce, ginger garlic paste, Chaney and many other things.I love this grinder. The wet and dry cups make this very versatile. I love the ease of cleaning out the cups. My old one was a hassle, this one is super easy to clean! I love the flexibility so much that I bought an additional wet and dry attachment and lid. I will probably pick up additional ones to give as chritmas gifts. This really is a fantastic grinder!!Bought a new one to replace my 4 year old broken one. This grinder is a workhorse, I use pretty much very day and the teeth on the motor side worn off.Make it good quality, grind coffeeGood grinder it has 2 stainless bowls for coffee beans and veggies strong enough for small quantities money/quality is great
reviews_1s:
I ordered this grinder as an extra. First off, the quality of the bowl is nice. No sharp edges like the KitchenAid. Bowl fits securely into the base, unlike the KitchenAid. The bowl for grinding spices is not the same as the previous model I purchased 19 Aug 2022. The redesigned model does not have the black pad below the blade. On the previous bowl, one blade tip faced up and the other down. On the newer one, both tips face up. I contacted Secura and was eventually sent a replacement bowl. It was the exact, redesigned, same thing. I was contacted by Secura via email "Thank you for providing the pictures. We believe it is exactly the correct replacement grinder bowl. The grinder bowl was slightly redesigned as some customers reported that they would like the black plastic pad removed. The new design improves grinding efficiency and it is easier to clean the bowl. Please let us know if we can be of any further assistance. Warm regards, Secura Customer Service TeamSpice G". Slightly? How many is some? The bowl was not hard to clean in the first place; soak and just use a toothbrush. It is not more efficient. It took me three times as long or longer to grind as before and still had small clumps. Did Secura even test their own redesign? Obviously not. The grinder was highly rated for what it was designed to do, grind spices, and the original bowl does an amazing job of it. It no longer does that. I received the copycat Shardor today hoping to get the bowl with the black pad. I did not receive the bowl with the black pad. Shardor replaced theirs with the same useless one as Secura. Shardor is going back to Amazon. Secura/Shardor, Please bring back the original grinder bowl like the one I purchased in Aug 2022, or at least make it an available option such as your Replacement Parts Stainless Steel Removable Bowl Set. I do not recommend this "redesigned" grinder bowl. Thank youI only used this last Spring to do 2 small batches of dried herbs, ground spices & nutritional yeast - all dry ingredients and now it is dead. What a waste of moneythey're working really well for dry stuff but when it comes to wet items they are a hassle. they get stuck on the sides of the containerWill be returing this product. I used the two blade cup to grind almonds and cashews and it took forever!!! After grinding for a while it could only remove the skin off of almonds. I had to chop it into smaller pieces to get almond mill.

It does grind the smaller spices fine though. But i bought this product to utilize it for everything and it doesn’t serve its purpose entirely.I bought this grinder and it overheated and the rubber in the bottom of the grinder got shaved and the grinder stopped rotating. I returned and got second grinder, and in 6 months the same issue happened. I will not recommend anyone to buy this product. I contacted them with my issue on their website and opened a ticket, but I have not received any information or follow-up.Not sure what happened - worked fine for a few months and then just stopped - it doesn't griond cinnamon sticks, cardamom pods, ect. Not sure why. Plus now it is overheating and the bowl gets incredibly hot. Junk... I have had it since February in a second home so it doesn't get tons of use.This is a faulty item. The motor of the machine started burning inside after using it only for three times.I bought this two years ago and used it occasionally. I was using it last night and there was smoke coming out of the bottom- essentially it got too hot and something burned in the circuit. I tried to use it after that but it was done for. I’m surprised that it overheated after barely using it. Does the company send replacements. Not sure what to do with the rest of the machineEven though used for less often, machine did not worked. Looking for replacement.
This is second time buying, wasting money. No services available.
Bad experience.LATEST (and last) REVIEW...

The machine lasted all of 3 months. There seem to be TWO fatal flaws with this product (from what I surmise wth my electrical engineering background):
#1 The motor is too weak. Although the bowl is very small, the motor is under-powered. (On one occasion I filled the bowl 3/4 full and after a few seconds the motor stopped. Some thermal fuse probably triggered because a few minutes later it restarted)
#2 The drive-train (from motor to bowl) is faulty. The 2nd (and final) time it stopped working the motor kept turning but the blades did not move.

PIECE OF JUNK!!!!

================================================================
EARLIER REVIEW (Upon initial use)...

ooks nice, steel bowl (not plastic), seems well-made.
Grinder works really well.

Nit-picks:
1 - The cover of the steel bowl looks like one of the zillions of disposable caps one sees in food containers. I am worried this may accidentally land in the trash someday.
2 - The wet bowl is a bit too small
3 - The dry bowl tends to leave some of the contents not ground. I need to stop and stir the bowl a couple of times during grinding.

But all told a very nice machine
